vocabulary.meaning: succeed in reaching a goal, especially by effort
vocabulary.example: She worked hard to achieve her goal of becoming a doctor.

vocabulary.meaning: organ of the body that controls thought and movement
vocabulary.example: The human brain is incredibly complex.

vocabulary.meaning: quick to learn and understand things
vocabulary.example: He is a clever boy who solves puzzles quickly.

vocabulary.meaning: give all your attention to something
vocabulary.example: Please be quiet; I need to concentrate on my homework.

vocabulary.meaning: think carefully about something before making a choice
vocabulary.example: We need to consider all options before deciding.

vocabulary.meaning: series of lessons or lectures on a particular subject
vocabulary.example: I am taking an English course this summer.

vocabulary.meaning: qualification given to a student after completing university
vocabulary.example: She has a bachelor's degree in chemistry.

vocabulary.meaning: knowledge or skill gained from doing things
vocabulary.example: Do you have any experience in customer service?

vocabulary.meaning: person with a high level of knowledge or skill in a subject
vocabulary.example: He is an expert in computer software.

vocabulary.meaning: not pass an exam or not succeed in doing something
vocabulary.example: He failed his driving test the first time.

vocabulary.meaning: give an answer without being sure it is correct
vocabulary.example: Can you guess how old he is?

vocabulary.meaning: pause before doing something because you are not sure
vocabulary.example: Don't hesitate to ask if you have any questions.

vocabulary.meaning: statement telling someone what to do or how to do it
vocabulary.example: Follow the instructions on the box carefully.

vocabulary.meaning: develop or improve in skills or knowledge
vocabulary.example: She is making great progress in her piano lessons.

vocabulary.meaning: check to be certain that something is correct or true
vocabulary.example: Make sure you lock the door before leaving.

vocabulary.meaning: score or grade given to a student's work
vocabulary.example: She got a high mark on her history test.

vocabulary.meaning: relating to the mind and thinking
vocabulary.example: Chess is a game that requires mental effort.

vocabulary.meaning: succeed in an exam or test
vocabulary.example: I hope I will pass my English exam.

vocabulary.meaning: official record showing you finished a training course
vocabulary.example: He has the right qualifications for the teaching job.

vocabulary.meaning: make someone remember something they have to do
vocabulary.example: Remind me to call the dentist tomorrow.

vocabulary.meaning: written or spoken description of a situation or event
vocabulary.example: She has to write a monthly report for her manager.

vocabulary.meaning: study materials again to prepare for an exam
vocabulary.example: I must revise my notes tonight for the test tomorrow.

vocabulary.meaning: look carefully in a place to find something
vocabulary.example: I searched the whole house for my keys.

vocabulary.meaning: ability to do something well because of practice
vocabulary.example: Writing is an important skill in communication.

vocabulary.meaning: clever and intelligent
vocabulary.example: She is a smart student who always gets good grades.

vocabulary.meaning: area of knowledge studied in school or university
vocabulary.example: Maths is my favorite subject.

vocabulary.meaning: do an official test of knowledge or ability
vocabulary.example: We have to take a final exam next week.

vocabulary.meaning: having a natural ability to do something well
vocabulary.example: She is a talented musician who plays three instruments.

vocabulary.meaning: one of the periods of time that a school year is divided into
vocabulary.example: The exams will take place at the end of this term.

vocabulary.meaning: ask yourself questions or want to know something
vocabulary.example: I wonder if it will rain tomorrow.

vocabulary.meaning: part of mathematics involving numbers and calculations
vocabulary.example: She excels at basic arithmetic like addition and multiplication.

vocabulary.meaning: scientific study of stars, planets, and space
vocabulary.example: He is interested in astronomy and bought a telescope.

vocabulary.meaning: study of the movements of stars to predict the future
vocabulary.example: She reads her horoscope every day because she likes astrology.

vocabulary.meaning: scientific test done to learn something or show a fact
vocabulary.example: We performed an experiment in the chemistry lab today.

vocabulary.meaning: science that studies substances and how they react
vocabulary.example: Chemistry lessons involve learning about elements and molecules.

vocabulary.meaning: sudden event that causes great damage or suffering
vocabulary.example: The earthquake was a terrible disaster for the town.

vocabulary.meaning: book or online tool that lists words and their meanings
vocabulary.example: You should look up unfamiliar words in a dictionary.

vocabulary.meaning: in a clean, tidy, and orderly way
vocabulary.example: She writes very neatly in her notebook.

vocabulary.meaning: with great attention and avoiding mistakes or danger
vocabulary.example: Drive carefully when the roads are wet.

vocabulary.meaning: piece of writing in short lines, often with rhyming words
vocabulary.example: She wrote a beautiful poem about spring.

vocabulary.meaning: short piece of writing about a subject, like an essay
vocabulary.example: The students were asked to write a composition about their holidays.

vocabulary.meaning: prize given to someone for an achievement
vocabulary.example: He received an award for being the best employee.

vocabulary.meaning: picture, short film, or text that tries to sell something
vocabulary.example: I saw an advertisement for a new smartphone on TV.

vocabulary.meaning: feeling fear or worry about something bad
vocabulary.example: Many young children are afraid of the dark.

vocabulary.meaning: something valuable given to the winner of a competition
vocabulary.example: She won first prize in the national spelling bee.

vocabulary.meaning: phrase used to express disappointment or regret
vocabulary.example: What a shame that you couldn't come to our party!

vocabulary.meaning: large reptile that lived millions of years ago
vocabulary.example: The museum has a complete skeleton of a dinosaur.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: draw a line through a word or mistake
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Just cross out the wrong word and write the correct one above it.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: find information in a book, dictionary, or online
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: You should look up the meaning of this word in the dictionary.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: show or tell someone an important fact
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The guide pointed out the historical monuments in the city.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: read words aloud so that other people can hear
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The teacher asked the student to read out the poem.
EX: She ripped up the old letters and threw them away.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: remove written marks using an eraser
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: You can rub out the pencil marks if they are incorrect.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: turn a page or object so the other side faces up
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Please turn over the test sheet when the exam begins.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: write information on paper so you don't forget it
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Write down his phone number before you forget it.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: from memory; learned so you can repeat it perfectly
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: She learned the whole poem by heart.
EX: Many countries, for instance, France and Italy, are famous for food.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: finally; as a final statement or summary
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: In conclusion, I would like to thank all of you for coming.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: actually; in reality (used to emphasize a fact)
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: He looks young, but in fact he is over fifty.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: supporting or agreeing with a plan or person
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: Most of the committee members were in favour of the proposal.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: usually; considering things as a whole
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: In general, the public transport in this city is very efficient.

wordFormation.meaning: begin / began / begun: start doing something / beginner: person who is starting to learn a skill / beginning: start of something
wordFormation.example:
EX: The concert will begin shortly.
EX: This course is suitable for complete beginners.
EX: The beginning of the film was very exciting.

wordFormation.meaning: brave: courageous; not afraid / bravery: courageous behavior
wordFormation.example:
EX: The brave firefighter saved the child.
EX: She was awarded a medal for her bravery.

wordFormation.meaning: correct: free from error; right / correction: change made to make something correct / incorrect: not correct; wrong
wordFormation.example:
EX: Please check if your answers are correct.
EX: The teacher made some corrections to my essay.
EX: His calculation was incorrect.

wordFormation.meaning: divide: separate into parts or groups / division: act of splitting something or a section of an organization
wordFormation.example:
EX: The teacher divided the class into four groups.
EX: The division of labor was equal among the team members.

wordFormation.meaning: educate: teach or train someone / education: process of teaching and learning, especially in schools
wordFormation.example:
EX: She was educated at a private school.
EX: Everyone has the right to a good education.

wordFormation.meaning: instruct: direct or teach someone / instruction: detailed information on how to do something / instructor: person who teaches a skill
wordFormation.example:
EX: The manager instructed the team on the new project.
EX: Read the instructions before using the machine.
EX: He works as a driving instructor.

wordFormation.meaning: memory: ability to remember things / memorise: learn something so that you remember it exactly / memorial: object built to honor a person or event
wordFormation.example:
EX: She has a very good memory for names.
EX: The students had to memorise the poem.
EX: A war memorial stands in the center of the town.

wordFormation.meaning: refer: direct someone's attention to something / reference: act of mentioning or looking at something for information
wordFormation.example:
EX: Please refer to the user manual for details.
EX: Keep this dictionary nearby for quick reference.

wordFormation.meaning: silent: quiet; without any sound / silence: complete lack of sound / silently: in a quiet way without speaking
wordFormation.example:
EX: The room was completely silent.
EX: They listened to the news in silence.
EX: She walked silently across the wooden floor.

wordFormation.meaning: simple: easy to understand or do / simplify: make something easier to understand / simplicity: quality of being easy to understand
wordFormation.example:
EX: The instructions are very simple to follow.
EX: We need to simplify the process to save time.
EX: I like the simplicity of this software's design.

wordPatterns.meaning: having the ability or power to do something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is capable of passing the exam easily.

wordPatterns.meaning: having a natural ability or skill in something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is talented at music.

wordPatterns.meaning: be dishonest in a game or test
wordPatterns.example: EX: He was caught cheating in the chemistry exam.

wordPatterns.meaning: mix up two things in your mind
wordPatterns.example: EX: People often confuse me with my twin brother.

wordPatterns.meaning: keep doing an activity without stopping
wordPatterns.example: EX: You should continue with your studies.

wordPatterns.meaning: deal successfully with a difficult situation
wordPatterns.example: EX: It can be hard to cope with exam stress.

wordPatterns.meaning: give assistance to someone with something
wordPatterns.example: EX: Can you help me with my geography project?

wordPatterns.meaning: have information or understanding of a subject
wordPatterns.example: EX: She knows a lot about astronomy.

wordPatterns.meaning: gain knowledge or information about something
wordPatterns.example: EX: We are learning about history in school today.

wordPatterns.meaning: achieve something that you have been trying to do
wordPatterns.example: EX: He succeeded in passing the difficult test.
EX: What is your opinion about the new school rules?

wordPatterns.meaning: sentence or phrase that asks for information
wordPatterns.example: EX: The student asked a question about the experiment.
